calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing And Motion For Writ Of Mandate; Memo P/A
Off Calendar Jun-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANGIE SCHWARTZ OF THE PUBLIC INTEREST LAW PROJECT, TEL. NO. (510) 891-9794 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. ELIZABETH EDWARDS, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5536 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PETITIONER JOSHUA LEONARD'S Motion For Writ Of Mandate IS GRANTED FRY CONTROLS THE DETERMINATION OF WHETHER THE COMPLETION RULE IS UNLAWFULLY DISCRIMATORY UNDER THE ADA. THE STATE HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT COMPLYING WITH THE COMPLETION RULE WOULD IMPOSE AN UNDUE FINANCIAL BURDEN. THE STATE HAS NOT CARRIED ITS FURTHER BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT IT CANNOT ABSORB THAT COST IN A REASONABLE WAY, NOTWITHSTANDING THE COURT RECOGNIZES THE SEVERE FINANCIAL CONSTRAINT UNDER WHICH THE STATE AND THE COUNTIES IN CALIFORNIA ARE OPERATING AT THIS POINT. THE COURT'S ORDER WILL BE TO DIRECT THE STATE TO COME FORWARD WITH A PLAN TO ADHERE AND CORRECT THE DISCRIMINATORY EFFECT OF THE COMPLETION RULE ON THOSE INDIVIDUALS BETWEEN THE AGES OF 18 AND 19 IN THE AFDC-FC PROGRAM WHO CAN DEMONSTRATE THAT THE REASON THEY CANNOT COMPLETE THE RELEVANT PROGRAM BY THE AGE OF 19 IS THEIR DISABILITY. THE STATE IS FREE TO ADOPT WHATEVER APPROPRIATE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEDURE IT CHOOSES TO CORRECT UNDER THE REASONING OF FRY THE DISPARATE TREATMENT OF THIS CLASS OF INDIVIDUALS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER WITH A TIME FRAME TO BE PLACED ON THE STATE PROPOSING AND IMPLEMENTING CORRECTIVE PROCEDURES. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/BD

Notice Of Hearing And Motion For Writ Of Mandate; Memo P/A
